active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 11 must target real evidence handoff/context selection failures after Phase 10, preserve v3 default/v1 fallback/kernel opt-in, require RED tests before code changes, and prove same-case movement through the v3/public benchmark path.

objective: Make selected, rendered, cited, and judged evidence handoff auditable and reliable for LoCoMo evidence-hit-answer-fail cases without hiding remaining retrieval misses or changing kernel defaults.

red_evidence_required_before_implementat.description: A selected expected source is dropped before final rendering and diagnostics must classify it as selected-drop/render-drop rather than generic evidence missing.
Rendered expected evidence must be visible to answer evidence construction with source id, session/date metadata, and rendered order.
Comparison paths must produce self-contained fail-to-pass/pass-to-fail movement fields or artifacts instead of relying on manual phase tables.
v1 fallback, v3 default, and kernel default-off behavior remain unchanged.

focused_verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_context_composer.py tests/test_public_benchmarks.py tests/test_public_failure_replay.py tests/test_agent_answer_eval.py -q

milestone_eval_commands.longmemeval_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark longmemeval --data-path benchmarks/longmemeval/longmemeval.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30 --llm-answer --llm-judge
milestone_eval_commands.locomo_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30 --llm-answer --llm-judge

usable_ack_checklist: Every Phase 11 artifact has phase binding.
Plan, execute, and review artifacts cite work/phase-11/context_bundle.md.
At least one evidence-hit-answer-fail or context handoff class improves or is converted into a more precise downstream class.
LoCoMo 30 has same-case explainable signal, not aggregate-only movement.
Pass-to-fail, fail-to-pass, unchanged-fail, retrieval/source movement, selected/rendered/cited movement, and failure-class movement are listed separately.
No case-id hacks, expected-answer leaks, scoring changes, v1 fallback regression, v3 default regression, or kernel-default change.
Review lane verdict is PASS or usable_ack before ACK.
review_focus: Does the implementation exercise the real v3/public benchmark path?
Did tests fail before handoff/context behavior changed?
Are selected, rendered, cited, and judged evidence statuses independently visible?
Are raw movement fields or comparison artifacts self-contained enough for same-case diagnosis?
Are remaining retrieval misses kept visible rather than hidden as answer failures?
Are source/retrieval metrics kept separate from judged answer quality?
Were LongMemEval and LoCoMo 30 gates run in parallel when required?
Were heartbeat/partial monitor artifacts updated for long evals?
Are pre-existing dirty AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and blueprint changes preserved?
